$comment: Codex CLI hook adapter (PLAN-L7-139). Repo-local ./hooks.json mirroring the Claude guard hooks in .claude/settings.json, but wired with Codex's real tool names. Reuses the SAME TypeScript entrypoints (no logic fork) - the guard logic lives in src/runtime/*.ts and is runtime-agnostic. work-guard extracts edit targets for BOTH Claude file_path and Codex apply_patch freeform patch bodies (multi-file), so the foreign-edit block actually fires for apply_patch (Codex's primary edit tool). NO global ~/.codex/ writes; this file is repo-relative and only effective when codex runs in this repository. Parity is machine-checked by `doctor` codex-hook-adapter (src/lint/codex-hook-adapter.ts). Codex tool-name mapping: Edit|Write|MultiEdit -> apply_patch|write_file ; Bash -> exec_command|local_shell. subagent-stop (SubagentStop) is genuinely N/A: codex.exe 0.128.0 has no SubagentStop event. agent-guard is NOT wired here yet: Codex DOES have a sub-agent surface (spawn_agent/wait_agent/list_agents tools), so an agent-guard analog is a deferred follow-up (a real, currently-unguarded surface), not an absent one.

hooks.statusMessage: work guard: block edits to foreign uncommitted files unless explicitly bypassed (Codex parity, PLAN-L7-139)
